heads_of_state.name: Richard Nixon
heads_of_state.background_detail: Lawyer, US Representative, US Senator, Vice President under Eisenhower (1953-1961)
key_economic_events: Ended Bretton Woods gold convertibility (Nixon Shock, 1971)

heads_of_state.name: Gerald Ford
heads_of_state.background_detail: Lawyer, US Representative from Michigan (1949-1973), House Minority Leader, Vice President (1973-1974), never elected to executive office

heads_of_state.name: Jimmy Carter
heads_of_state.background_detail: Naval officer and nuclear engineer, peanut farmer, Governor of Georgia (1971-1975)
key_economic_events: Appointed Paul Volcker as Fed Chair (1979)

heads_of_state.name: Ronald Reagan
heads_of_state.background_detail: Actor, President of Screen Actors Guild, Governor of California (1967-1975)

heads_of_state.name: George H. W. Bush
heads_of_state.background_detail: Navy pilot, oil businessman, US Representative, UN Ambassador, CIA Director (1976-1977), Vice President under Reagan (1981-1989)
key_economic_events: Budget deal with tax increases (broke 'read my lips' pledge)

heads_of_state.name: Bill Clinton
heads_of_state.background_detail: Law professor, Attorney General of Arkansas, Governor of Arkansas (1979-1981, 1983-1992)

heads_of_state.name: George W. Bush
heads_of_state.background_detail: Oil business executive, Managing General Partner of Texas Rangers baseball team, Governor of Texas (1995-2000)
key_economic_events: Post-9/11 economic disruption and war spending
Bear Stearns and Lehman Brothers collapse

heads_of_state.name: Barack Obama
heads_of_state.background_detail: Constitutional law professor, community organizer, Illinois State Senator (1997-2004), US Senator from Illinois (2005-2008)
key_economic_events: Slow but steady recovery, unemployment fell from 10% to 4.7%

heads_of_state.name: Donald Trump
heads_of_state.background_detail: Real estate mogul, CEO of The Trump Organization, TV personality (The Apprentice), no prior political or military office
key_economic_events: Federal Reserve emergency rate cuts to near zero
Stock market crash and rapid V-shaped recovery

heads_of_state.name: Joe Biden
heads_of_state.background_detail: Lawyer, US Senator from Delaware (1973-2009), Vice President under Obama (2009-2017)
key_economic_events: Post-COVID inflation surge, CPI peaked at 9.1% (June 2022)
Silicon Valley Bank collapse and regional banking crisis (2023)
Strong labor market despite rate hikes
Russia-Ukraine war economic sanctions and energy price impact

heads_of_state.name: Donald Trump
heads_of_state.background_detail: Former President (2017-2021), real estate mogul, first president to serve non-consecutive terms since Grover Cleveland
key_economic_events: Renewed tariff escalation on China, EU, and allies
Government efficiency and spending reduction initiatives (DOGE)
Market volatility from trade policy uncertainty

central_bank.name: Arthur Burns
central_bank.background_detail: Economics professor at Columbia University, Chair of Council of Economic Advisers under Eisenhower (1953-1956), NBER president
key_economic_events: Nixon Shock / end of Bretton Woods (1971)

central_bank.name: G. William Miller
central_bank.background_detail: CEO of Textron conglomerate, lawyer, no prior central banking experience; later became Treasury Secretary (1979-1981)
key_economic_events: Dollar crisis on foreign exchange markets
Widely regarded as ineffective; replaced by Volcker

central_bank.name: Paul Volcker
central_bank.background_detail: Career public servant, Treasury Department official, President of Federal Reserve Bank of New York (1975-1979)
key_economic_events: Volcker Shock: raised federal funds rate to 20% to crush inflation

central_bank.name: Alan Greenspan
central_bank.party_or_affiliation: Republican-appointed (reappointed by Clinton and Bush W)
central_bank.background_detail: Private economic consultant, founder of Townsend-Greenspan & Co., Chair of Council of Economic Advisers under Ford (1974-1977)
key_economic_events: Black Monday response (1987) — provided liquidity, stabilized markets
Low interest rate policy fueled housing bubble
Criticized for light-touch regulation of derivatives

central_bank.name: Ben Bernanke
central_bank.background_detail: Economics professor at Princeton, leading scholar of Great Depression, Fed Governor (2002-2005), Chair of Council of Economic Advisers (2005-2006), Nobel Prize in Economics (2022)
key_economic_events: 2008 global financial crisis — emergency lending facilities
Bear Stearns rescue, Lehman Brothers collapse
Near-zero interest rate policy (ZIRP) for 7 years
Established forward guidance as major policy tool

central_bank.name: Janet Yellen
central_bank.background_detail: Economics professor at UC Berkeley, Fed Governor (1994-1997), Chair of Council of Economic Advisers (1997-1999), President of SF Fed (2004-2010), Fed Vice Chair (2010-2014); first woman to chair the Fed; later Treasury Secretary (2021-2025)
key_economic_events: First rate hike in nearly a decade (December 2015)
Low inflation puzzle — below 2% target despite low unemployment
Smooth transition away from crisis-era policies

central_bank.name: Jerome Powell
central_bank.background_detail: Lawyer, investment banker at Dillon Read and Carlyle Group, Treasury Undersecretary under George H.W. Bush, Fed Governor (2012-2018); first non-economist to chair the Fed since the 1980s
key_economic_events: 2018 rate hikes and Trump's public criticism of Fed
COVID-19 emergency response: rates to zero, unlimited QE (March 2020)
Most aggressive rate hike cycle since Volcker (2022-2023): 0% to 5.25-5.50%
Silicon Valley Bank and regional banking stress (2023)
Navigating Trump second-term political pressure on Fed independence
